etymology.description: semantic 頁(頁) (yè, "page") + phonetic 予(予) (yǔ) -> anticipated response or preparation
etymology.interpretation: 頁(頁) (yè, "page") suggests information or a plan, while 予(予) (yǔ) provides the sound, leading to the idea of preparation or arranging in advance. The concept of preparing or planning naturally precedes an event, reflecting the meaning 'in advance' or 'prepare'.
etymology.simplification: Simplified to 预 by reducing components 頁(頁) to 页, retaining phonetic 予(予).

examples.chinese: 他提前通知了我们(他提前通知了我們)，让我们做好准备(讓我們做好準備)。
examples.pinyin: Tā tíqián tōngzhī le wǒmen, ràng wǒmen zuò hǎo zhǔnbèi.
examples.english: He informed us in advance, letting us prepare well.

examples.chinese: 他们正在预热机器(他們正在預熱機器)。
会议已经预定了时间和地点(會議已經預定了時間和地點)。
examples.pinyin: Huìyì yǐjīng yùdìng le shíjiān hé dìdiǎn.
examples.english: The meeting has already set the time and location beforehand.
